title: FromStr
description: The `FromStr` trait allows parsing a type from a string. It's what powers the `.parse()` method.
instructions: Implement FromStr for a custom type to enable parsing with .parse().
hints: Strip the parentheses first with trim_start_matches and trim_end_matches.
Split by ',' and parse each part as u8.
Return an error if there aren't exactly 3 components or parsing fails.
explanation: Implementing `FromStr` lets your type work with the `.parse()` method on strings. The trait has an associated `Err` type for parse failures. The `?` operator with `map_err` converts the inner parse errors to your error type. This pattern is common for creating types that need to be deserialized from text.
